title: Preferential Lysis of S. rosetta for Total RNA (VERSION 2)
author: David Booth

steps_raw.instruction: Combine the following components for the lysis buffer: A B C D E 1 Chemical [Final] [Stock] Final Vol Chemical Vol 2 Water 10 ml 0.41 ml 3 Tris-HCl, pH 8.0 20 mM 1 M 200 µl 4 KCl 150 mM 2 M 750 µl 5 MgCl2 5 mM 1 M 50 µl 6 Sucrose 250 mM 1.75 M (60% w/v) 1ml 420 uL 7 Cycloheximide 100 ug/ml 100 mg/ml 10 ul 8 Protease inhibitor tablet 2 mini tablet / 10 ml 1 ml 9 Digitonin 10 mM 20 mM 5 ml 10 Sodium Heparin 1 mg/ml 100 mg/ml 100 µl 11 Pefabloc SC 1 mM 200 mM 50 µl 12 DTT 1 mM 1 M 10 µl 13 Turbo DNase 0.1 U/ml 2 U/µl 500 µl 14 SUPERaseIN 1 U/ml 20 U/µl 500 µl Notes: 1. Prepare the buffer ahead of time by combining all but the italicized reagents (DTT, Turbo DNase, and SUPERaseIn), splitting into 449.5 µl aliquots, and storing at -20°C. 2. Just before use, thaw the prepared lysis buffer on ice and then add 0.5 µl of 1 M DTT, 25 µl of Turbo DNase, and 25 µl SUPERaseIn to the 449.5 µl aliquot for a total volume of 500 µl.
Determine the total number of cells in the culture that will be harvested using a hemocytometer.
Fix 200 µl of cells with 2 µl of 37% formaldeyde and vortex well.
Pipet up and down to homogenize cells, and pipet 12 µl of cells into the chamber of a hemocytometer.
Count the number of cells ( N ) in the four corner quandrants of a Neubauer, bright-line hemocytometer.
Calculate the cell concentration ( [Cells] cells/ml) according to this equation: [Cells]=N/4 • 10000 cells/ml
Determine the volume of the culture ( Vol ) and then calculate the total number of cells in the culture ( Total ) according to this equation: Total=[cells]•Vol cells
Calculate the volume of lysis buffer ( LysisBuffer µl) to add to the cells for lysis: LysisBuffer=Total/100000 µl
Centrifuge the cells in 50 ml conical tubes at 2400 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes
Gently remove supernatant with a serological pipette, leaving a small amount of liquid of the pellet.
Gently remove the remaining supernatant with a fine-tip transfer pipet.
Lyse cells in preferential lysis buffer.
Resuspend the cell pellet ( go to step #3.3 ) in the calculated volume of LysisBuffer ( go to step #2.6 ).
Pipet the cells gently up and down and then incubate On ice for 00:10:00 .
Homogenize the lysate by passing 5 times through a 30G needle attached to a luer lock syringe.
Clarify the lysate by centrifugation 6000 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes
Separate the S. rosetta lysate from the bacterial pellet by using a gel-loading tip to gently transfer the supernatant into a new tube. Pay attention not to disturb the pellet.
Flash freeze lysate in liquid nitrogen and store at -80°C for long term storage.
